- `javascriptallonge-nested-blocks` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0050-0001` [limitation] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.5` terms `now, only, ever, seen` statement `Up to now, we've only ever seen blocks we use as the body of functions.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0050-0008` [identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.5` terms `statement, not, expression, unfortunate` statement `The if statement is a statement, not an expression (an unfortunate design choice), and its clauses are statements or blocks.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0050-0012` [method] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.0` terms `block, used, else, clause` statement `We've used a block as the else clause, and since it's a block, we've placed a const statement inside it.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0050-0003` [identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.5` terms `one, places, you, find` statement `One of the places you can find blocks is in an if statement.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0050-0002` [identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.5` terms `there, other, kinds, blocks` statement `But there are other kinds of blocks.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.54-55` in every bullet_text; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.54-55` | path `javascriptallonge-nested-blocks.md`
- `javascriptallonge-const-and-lexical-scope` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0051-0020` [relationship] eligibility `eligible` centrality `1.0` terms `binding, values, names, const` statement `Binding values to names with const works just like binding values to names with parameter invocations, it uses lexical scope.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0051-0001` [identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.333` terms `seems, very, straightforward, alas` statement `This seems very straightforward, but alas, there are some semantics of binding names that we need to understand if we're to place const anywhere we like.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.55-57` in every bullet_text; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.55-57` | path `javascriptallonge-const-and-lexical-scope.md`

- `javascriptallonge-function-declaration-caveats-34` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0009` [limitation, identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.667` terms `function, expression, another, caveat` statement `Another caveat is that a function declaration cannot exist inside of any expression, otherwise it's a function expression.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0001` [ordinary-modality, limitation, function, identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.333` terms `function, declarations, formally, only` statement `Function declarations are formally only supposed to be made at what we might call the 'top level' of a function.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0006` [identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.333` terms `function, declarations, not, supposed` statement `Function declarations are not supposed to occur inside of blocks.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0003` [quantitative] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.667` terms `number, caveats, discussed, here` statement `34 A number of the caveats discussed here were described in Jyrly Zaytsev's excellent article Named function expressions demystified.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0011` [unlabeled] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.667` terms `parentheses, make, expression, not` statement `The parentheses make this an expression, not a function declaration.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.66-67` in every bullet_text; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.66-67` | path `javascriptallonge-function-declaration-caveats-34.md`
- `javascriptallonge-higher-order-functions` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0059-0003` [function, identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `1.0` terms `function, generally, speaking, either` statement `Generally speaking, a function that either takes functions as arguments, or returns a function, or both, is referred to as a 'higher-order' function.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0059-0002` [function, identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.5` terms `functions, javascript, values, take` statement `JavaScript functions are values, so JavaScript functions can take functions as arguments, return functions, or both.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0059-0001` [function] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.5` terms `values, seen, javascript, functions` statement `As we've seen, JavaScript functions take values as arguments and return values.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0059-0008` [unlabeled] eligibility `eligible` centrality `1.0` terms `talk, some, specific, types` statement `But before we go on, we'll talk about some specific types of higher-order functions.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0059-0007` [unlabeled] eligibility `eligible` centrality `1.0` terms `higher-order, functions, dominate, javascript` statement `Higher-order functions dominate JavaScript Allongé .` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.68-68` in every bullet_text; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.68-68` | path `javascriptallonge-higher-order-functions.md`

- `javascriptallonge-functions-are-not-the-real-point` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0142-0008` [relationship, identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.5` terms `knowing, how, make, linked` statement `Knowing how to make a linked list out of functions is not really necessary for the working programmer.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0142-0001` [identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.25` terms `there, lots, similar, texts` statement `There are lots of similar texts explaining how to construct complex semantics out of functions.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0142-0012` [unlabeled] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.25` terms `deeply, important, not, practical` statement `Deeply important, but not practical when you're building a bridge.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0142-0010` [quantitative, identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.25` terms `knowing, how, make, list` statement `Knowing how to make a list out of just functions is a little like knowing that photons are the Gauge Bosons 81 of the electromagnetic force.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0142-0004` [identity] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.25` terms `functions, fundamental, building, block` statement `Functions are a fundamental building block of computation.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.188-189` in every bullet_text; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.188-189` | path `javascriptallonge-functions-are-not-the-real-point.md`
- `javascriptallonge-a-return-to-backward-thinking` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0143-0001` [function] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.0` terms `pair, make, pairs, work` statement `To make pairs work, we did things backwards , we passed the first and rest functions to the pair, and the pair called our function.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0143-0003` [ordinary-modality, attribute] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.0` terms `could, done, something, completely` statement `But we could have done something completely different.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0143-0004` [ordinary-modality, attribute] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.0` terms `pair, stored, its, elements` statement `We could have written a pair that stored its elements in an array, or a pair that stored its elements in a POJO.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.189-190` in every bullet_text; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.189-190` | path `javascriptallonge-a-return-to-backward-thinking.md`

- `javascriptallonge-representing-naughts-and-crosses-as-a-stateful-function` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0192-0003` [mechanism] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.4` terms `case, need, stateful, function` statement `In that case, we need a stateful function.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0192-0004` [provenance, function] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.2` terms `game, function, our, will` statement `Our 'API' will work like this: When we want a new game, we'll call a function that will return a game function, We'll call the game function repeatedly,...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0192-0001` [function] eligibility `eligible` centrality `0.2` terms `our, statelessnaughtsandcrosses, function, pushes` statement `Our statelessNaughtsAndCrosses function pushes the work of tracking the game's state onto us, the player.` | constraints: put citation `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.279-280` in every bullet_text; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.279-280` | path `javascriptallonge-representing-naughts-and-crosses-as-a-stateful-function.md`
